india_trends.title: Netweb Technologies Q4 results: Revenue leaps 90%, profit after tax jumps to ₹706 million, up 66% YoY — Key highlights
india_trends.summary: Netweb Technologies, provider for AMD, Intel, Nvidia and Samsung, called FY26 a “landmark” year, with full-year revenue jumping 90% YoY to ₹21,836 million. PAT for the quarter stood at ₹706 million, representing 65.7% YoY growth.

india_trends.title: France seeks enforceable rules in India-EU FTA talks
india_trends.summary: In a move to strengthen economic ties, France is pushing for actionable measures to eliminate trade obstacles between India and the European Union. Minister Delegate Nicolas Forissier emphasized the urgency of addressing non-tariff challenges. With the recent Free Trade Agreement in place, the aim i

india_trends.title: Government extends minimum import price on Multi-Layer Paper Board till September 30
india_trends.summary: The government has extended the minimum import price (MIP) on virgin multi-layer paper board at ₹67,220 per metric tonne until September 30, 2026, according to an official notification.

india_trends.title: DEA notifies FDI easing for foreign cos with up to 10 pc Chinese stake under FEMA
india_trends.summary: New rules allow overseas firms with up to 10 percent Chinese ownership to invest in India automatically. This applies to sectors open for foreign direct investment. However, companies registered in China, Hong Kong, or countries sharing a land border with India are excluded. These changes aim to str

india_trends.title: Berkshire Hathaway profit rises despite consumer stress, cash sets record
india_trends.summary: Berkshire Hathaway reported stronger Q1 operating profit despite economic headwinds impacting consumer businesses. india_trends.title: AI risks keep me up at night, says Kotak Bank CEO
india_trends.summary: Kotak Mahindra Bank Q4 profit rose 13%, but CEO flagged rising AI-driven cyber risks.

india_trends.title: Jindal Steel, Thyssenkrupp pause talks on steel unit stake
india_trends.summary: Thyssenkrupp AG and Jindal Steel International have paused discussions on a potential stake sale in the German firm’s steel unit. india_trends.title: India should create strategic buffers to navigate 'most difficult' energy shock: CEA Nageswaran
india_trends.summary: V Anantha Nageswaran has called for India to build strategic buffers to manage the ongoing energy shock triggered by the West Asia crisis, warning it is the most severe in recent times.

india_trends.title: CDSL Q4 profit slips to ₹80 cr, demat accounts cross 18 cr
india_trends.summary: For the full 2025-26 fiscal, net profit dips to ₹455 crore from ₹526 crore in the previous 2024-25 financial year

india_trends.title: Sony Pictures Networks India hires Harsh Deep Chhabra to lead strategy, new business
india_trends.summary: Sony Pictures Networks India has appointed Harsh Deep Chhabra as Head of Strategy and New Business, with the executive joining from Godrej Consumer Products Limited.

india_trends.title: Rohit Jain appointed RBI deputy governor
india_trends.summary: Rohit Jain, a Reserve Bank of India executive director, has been appointed deputy governor. His term begins on or after May 3, 2026, and will last for three years. Jain has served the central bank for three decades. He replaces T Rabi Sankar. Jain is one of two deputy governors promoted from within

india_trends.title: DMart Q4 Results: Avenue Supermarts cons PAT jumps 19% to Rs 656 crore, revenue too rises 19%
india_trends.summary: DMart Q4 Results: Avenue Supermarts, the operator of DMart, on Saturday reported a net profit of Rs 656.6 crore for the March quarter of FY26, marking a jump of 19% from Rs 550.90 crore posted in the corresponding quarter of the previous financial year. The net profit is attributable to the equity h

india_trends.title: World’s largest container carrier plans route avoiding Hormuz
india_trends.summary: The world’s largest container shipping company, MSC Mediterranean Shipping Company, is launching a new Europe–Middle East service that avoids the Strait of Hormuz by combining sea, road, and feeder ship routes.

india_trends.title: FPIs pull out ₹13,944 crore in holiday-shortened week, equities hit hardest
india_trends.summary: While outflows showed signs of moderation towards the end of the week, global headwinds, rising crude prices, and geopolitical tensions continued to weigh on sentiment

india_trends.title: Specialised investment funds see rapid growth, attract beyond top 30 cities
india_trends.summary: AUM has more than doubled to ₹10,620 crore, with fund houses launching new offerings and eyeing ₹1 lakh crore in assets over the next three years, despite distribution challenges

india_trends.title: India notifies 100% foreign investment in insurance sector, LIC limit stays at 20%
india_trends.summary: The central government has notified 100% Foreign Direct Investment (FDI) in insurance companies under the automatic route, allowing foreign investors to fully invest in the sector without prior approvals, according to a report by ET Now citing agencies.

india_trends.title: India eases HVDC localisation rules, sets timeline to reach 60% local content by 2035
india_trends.summary: The Ministry of Power has updated its 'Make in India' rules for the power sector. A new plan will gradually increase local content in HVDC substations. This aims for 60 percent Indian-made components by 2035. The changes provide a transition for domestic industries to grow their capacity.

india_trends.title: CDSL Q4 Results: Cons PAT declines 20% to Rs 80 crore, Rs 12.75/share dividend declared
india_trends.summary: CDSL Q4 Results: Central Depository Services Limited reported a mixed Q4 FY26 performance, with revenue growing 17.1% to Rs 262.85 crore, but net profit declining 20% to Rs 80.22 crore. The company surpassed 18 crore demat accounts, highlighting growing investor trust despite profitability pressures

india_trends.title: FII outflows not driven by lack of AI and high taxes, says Shankar Sharma. Here’s why
india_trends.summary: Foreign investors are not avoiding India due to AI exposure or tax issues, according to investor Shankar Sharma. Data shows markets with little AI have outperformed. India's own past performance contradicts the AI dependency narrative. FII flows and taxation show a weak link to market direction. Glo
